scope.summary: Implement topstitch as exported 3D polygon geometry: extrude internal guide lines (MT-132) into a thin ribbon mesh offset above the garment surface, apply smooth-corner beveling at path joints, and include the ribbon mesh in the glTF/USD production export package alongside the main garment mesh
acceptance_criteria: Internal guide lines tagged as topstitch in PanelSpec (via a TopstitchLine struct with width_mm and thread_color) generate a ribbon mesh with configurable width (default 1.5 mm) offset 0.1 mm above the garment surface normal
Corner joints in topstitch paths are beveled (smooth corner) rather than miter-clipped, matching MD 2025.0 Smooth Corner for Topstitch behavior
The topstitch ribbon mesh is included as a named child mesh in the glTF export (MT-249) and the USD export (MT-250) with its own PBR material slot (thread color + roughness)
Polygon count around topstitch geometry is bounded (polygon optimization pass): no topstitch ribbon exceeds 6 quads per centimeter of path length
The render viewport (MT-236) displays topstitch ribbon geometry in the standard PBR pass, togglable via the visual-debug overlay system (MT-245)
scope.risk_if_missed: Topstitch is a primary visual quality marker in fashion garments — visible stitching lines on jeans, outerwear, and sportswear are the difference between a production-quality garment and a smooth toy model. Texture-only topstitch (MT-226) looks flat and baked; geometry topstitch catches lighting and reads as real stitching in renders and real-time engines. Missing this makes exported garments visually inadequate for production review and game-engine use.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
